summary: Blazing Dark Star, tight Eleven, and soaring jams all night
blurb: The set rides a psychedelic wave from acoustic Dupree's into an expansive Dark Star, then into The Eleven and an Alligator/Feedback climax. Jerry delivers a blazing guitar tone with one of his best Dark Star solos, while Phil’s bass drives the groove throughout. Highlights include Mountains Of The Moon, Saint Stephen > Turn On Your Lovelight, and Pigpen’s organ on Cryptical Envelopment.
review: April 5, 1969 at the Avalon Ballroom captured a night when the Dead were truly in their psychedelic stride. Opening with Dupree's Diamond Blues and a soaring Mountains Of The Moon, the band melted into a monster Dark Star that reviewers called “blazing” and “fire‑y,” featuring Jerry Garcia’s arguably best solo of the era. Seamless segues carried the set through Saint Stephen into a relentless Eleven where Phil Lesh was “all over” the groove and the dual drummers formed a “six‑armed creature” of counter‑rhythms. The second set built to an intense Alligator/Feedback climax, punctuated by Pigpen’s organ on Cryptical Envelopment and a jubilant turn into The Other One. Across all three soundboard sources the performance is described as crystal‑clear, tight, and “on fire,” making this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that late‑'60s psychedelic peak.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are positive, repeatedly praising the band's tightness, Jerry's blazing guitar, Phil's driving bass, and the powerful Eleven and Dark Star jams. Consistent language like 'monster Dark Star', 'crystal‑clear audio', and 'tight counter‑rhythms' indicates strong collective enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, superb instrument separation and full set coverage; widely praised as the clearest representation of this performance.

key_highlights: Monster Dark Star with one of Jerry's best solos.
The Eleven showcases a tight, six‑armed drum groove and Phil’s bass dominance.
Alligator/Feedback climax delivers raw psychedelic intensity.
Pigpen’s organ on Cryptical Envelopment adds a soaring, carousel‑like texture.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is blazing and fire‑y, delivering a crystal‑clear guitar that shines especially on Dark Star.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh is ‘all over’ the performance, driving the groove during The Eleven and The Other One with relentless bass energy.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s early rhythm work adds flavor despite some developing rough edges; vocal cracks are noted but minor.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart, together with Kreutzmann, forms a six‑armed creature of tight counter‑rhythms that propels the band especially in The Eleven and Feedback.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matches Hart’s intensity, delivering crisp, interlocking drum patterns throughout the set.

must_listen_sequences: And We Bid You Good Night
